display.purpose: The nation name on the passport, and landing hero. Anton, towering and condensed. Always the largest element on screen.

headline.purpose: Section openers and the landing call. Anton, large but below Display.

title.purpose: Quiz questions and answer-tile labels. Hanken Grotesk, confident and readable.

body.purpose: The 'why you' paragraph and explanatory UI. Hanken Grotesk, max 65-70ch, extra line-height on dark.

label.purpose: Archetype kicker, passport data fields, CTA text, tags. Martian Mono, small, tracked, uppercase.

shadows.name: flare-bloom
shadows.purpose: Warm colored glow under the primary CTA on hover. A light source, not a gray shadow.

shadows.name: nation-bloom
shadows.purpose: Blooms in behind the passport at the peak of the reveal (uses --nation-primary at runtime). The poster is lit from behind.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for reveals and transitions. No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Softer alternative for micro-interactions and state changes.

motion.name: dur-standard
motion.purpose: Question advance, tile transitions, button state.

motion.name: dur-reveal-beat
motion.purpose: One staggered beat of the passport reveal choreography (type settles, color floods, grain resolves).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one call to action on a chrome screen. Signal Flare, short Martian Mono label.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Martian Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(18% 0.012 65); background: oklch(67% 0.21 35); border: none; border-radius: 12px; padding: 18px 36px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(58% 0.20 32); transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 8px 40px oklch(67% 0.21 35 / 0.35); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(67% 0.21 35); outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary actions: download, skip, back. Bordered, transparent.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: 'Martian Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(92% 0.008 80); background: transparent; border: 1px solid oklch(34% 0.012 65); border-radius: 12px; padding: 18px 36px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(23% 0.013 65); border-color: oklch(62% 0.01 70); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(67% 0.21 35); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Answer Tile
components.description: A full-width editorial choice row in the quiz, on Cinema Lift so it reads as tappable on the dark field. A tap auto-advances, so there is no persistent selected state; the tap itself is the feedback. Text-led, stacked, never an icon grid.
components.css: .ds-chip-answer { display: flex; align-items: center; width: 100%; text-align: left; min-height: 60px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', 'Noto Sans', sans-serif; font-size: clamp(1.05rem, 3.6vw, 1.25rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.15; color: oklch(92% 0.008 80); background: oklch(26.5% 0.014 65); border: 1px solid oklch(42% 0.013 65); border-radius: 14px; padding: 17px 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), border-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-chip-answer:hover { background: oklch(31% 0.015 65); border-color: oklch(50% 0.06 45); } .ds-chip-answer:active { color: oklch(97% 0.006 85); transform: scale(0.985); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px oklch(67% 0.21 35); } .ds-chip-answer:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(67% 0.21 35); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Reveal Suspense (signature)
components.description: The labor-illusion loader between the last quiz answer and the passport: a 9:16 poster skeleton on Cinema Lift with a printing shimmer and a faint flare bloom, under a cycling Martian Mono status line. Visible effort reads as more valuable, safe only because every nation lands as a hero.
components.css: .ds-suspense { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 26px; } .ds-suspense-skeleton { position: relative; width: clamp(150px, 42vw, 190px); aspect-ratio: 9 / 16; border-radius: 18px; overflow: hidden; background: oklch(26% 0.013 65); border: 1px solid oklch(40% 0.012 65); box-shadow: 0 24px 80px oklch(67% 0.21 35 / 0.12); } .ds-suspense-skeleton::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: linear-gradient(110deg, transparent 28%, oklch(45% 0.02 65 / 0.55) 50%, transparent 72%); transform: translateX(-110%); animation: ds-suspense-shimmer 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-suspense-shimmer { to { transform: translateX(110%); } } .ds-suspense-line { font-family: 'Martian Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.82rem; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(92% 0.008 80); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-suspense-skeleton::after { animation: none; } }

components.name: Email Field
components.description: The optional 'save your passport' upgrade. Quiet by default, never a gate.
components.html: <input class="ds-input-email" type="email" placeholder="you@email.com" aria-label="Email to save your passport" />
components.css: .ds-input-email { font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', 'Noto Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 1.0625rem; color: oklch(92% 0.008 80); background: oklch(23% 0.013 65); border: 1px solid oklch(34% 0.012 65); border-radius: 12px; padding: 16px 18px; width: 100%; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-input-email::placeholder { color: oklch(62% 0.01 70); } .ds-input-email:focus { outline: none; border-color: oklch(67% 0.21 35); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(67% 0.21 35 / 0.4); }

components.name: Supporter Passport (signature)
components.description: The drenched 9:16 poster the whole product compresses into. Nation duotone floods the surface; the Anton name towers; Martian Mono data band reads like a ticket stub.
components.html:
THE STREET POETS

ARGENTINA

NO. 04127GROUP CPASSION 92
components.css: .ds-passport { position: relative; aspect-ratio: 9 / 16; width: 320px; max-width: 100%; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 20px; background: radial-gradient(120% 90% at 50% 18%, var(--nation-primary), var(--nation-deep) 70%); box-shadow: 0 24px 120px var(--nation-primary); } .ds-passport-grain { position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.10; background-image: radial-gradient(oklch(100% 0 0) 0.5px, transparent 0.5px); background-size: 3px 3px; mix-blend-mode: overlay; } .ds-passport::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 140px 30px oklch(18% 0.012 65 / 0.55); } .ds-passport-body { position: relative; height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: flex-end; padding: 28px 24px; } .ds-passport-kicker { font-family: 'Martian Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--nation-glow); } .ds-passport-name { font-family: 'Anton', 'Arial Narrow', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: clamp(3.5rem, 22vw, 5.5rem); line-height: 0.86; letter-spacing: -0.01em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--nation-glow); margin: 4px 0 18px; } .ds-passport-stub { display: flex; gap: 14px; flex-wrap: wrap; padding: 12px 14px; border-radius: 12px; background: oklch(18% 0.012 65 / 0.28); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border: 1px solid oklch(92% 0.008 80 / 0.18); font-family: 'Martian Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.65rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: oklch(92% 0.008 80); }

narrative.overview: Bandwagon is where the football terrace meets the movie poster. The terrace gives it warmth, noise, and belonging; the cinema gives it scale, darkness, and the held breath before the reveal. The whole system lives in a warm cinema-black room, and every path through it ends on one screen: a Supporter Passport so big and so drenched in your nation's color that posting it feels like releasing a poster for the team you just joined. The chrome is the dim warm lobby (cinema-black, one Signal Flare accent, towering condensed type); the passport is the screen (a nation's duotone floods the surface, the country name stands monumentally tall, the data reads like a ticket stub). Two rooms, one building; the lobby never competes with the screen.
keyCharacteristics: Warm cinema-black world, dark-mode-first, never pure black or pure white.
Two-register split: restrained Committed chrome, fully Drenched per-nation passport.
Towering condensed display type as the dominant visual force.
The artifact is the product: every flow ends in a screenshot-worthy poster.
Choreographed reveal (suspense, then identity, then pride), all on ease-out-expo.
Every nation hero-worthy: the design dignifies debutants and giants alike.

rules.name: The Two Rooms Rule
rules.body: The chrome is the lobby; the passport is the screen. Signal Flare lives in the lobby and is forbidden on the passport hero. The nation duotone lives on the screen and is forbidden as chrome decoration.

rules.name: The Drench Rule
rules.body: On the passport, the nation does not get a stripe, a badge, or a corner. It gets the whole surface. Nation Deep floods the full bleed; Nation Primary and Glow carry the type.

rules.name: The Flare Rarity Rule
rules.body: Signal Flare covers a small fraction of any chrome screen. Its scarcity is what makes the CTA read as the one thing to do.

rules.name: The Monument Rule
rules.body: The nation name is always the biggest thing on screen and always Anton, fit to span the poster width. It does not share the stage with another element at the same scale.

rules.name: The Solid-Color Rule
rules.body: Type is one solid color, always. Gradient text is forbidden. Emphasis comes from scale, weight, and color choice.

rules.name: The No-Box-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Gray drop shadows are forbidden. Depth is grain, vignette, tonal layering, and colored bloom. The only shadows permitted are the flare bloom and the nation bloom, and only on a hero moment.

rules.name: The Tappable-Lift Rule
rules.body: Interactive tiles sit on Cinema Lift (a step brighter than Cinema Raise), never raw Cinema Raise. The extra lightness is what separates 'tap me' from a passive panel on a near-black field.

rules.name: The Immersive-Hide Rule
rules.body: On the full-bleed surfaces (the quiz and the passport reveal), the app nav is removed entirely so nothing competes with the show. It returns on the lobby surfaces.

rules.name: The Export Fit Rule
rules.body: html-to-image rasterizes the masthead in a fallback font roughly 9% wider than Anton, so the export instance fits the name to 88% of width and never clips, while on-screen stays full-width Anton.

dos: Do drench the passport in the nation's duotone (Nation Deep full bleed, Nation Primary and Glow type). The whole surface, never a stripe or a corner.
Do set the nation name monumentally in Anton as the largest element on screen.
Do keep Signal Flare to the chrome and scarce (the One CTA), and keep the nation duotone to the passport.
Do render supporter traits as editorial count-up numerals woven into the poster.
Do use OKLCH and tint every neutral warm; pair every flag color with the nation name, crest, and a text label so meaning never rests on color alone (WCAG 2.2 AA).
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: the suspense loader, confetti, count-ups, and tilt all collapse to a dignified instant state.
Do animate only transform and opacity, on ease-out-expo / quint, no bounce.
Do gate the visible-effort suspense to a guaranteed-flattering result: the labor illusion lifts perceived value only when the payoff lands as a great, earned match.
Do hide the app nav on the immersive surfaces (/quiz, /p/*); seat interactive tiles on Cinema Lift, never raw Cinema Raise.
donts: Don't make a cheesy BuzzFeed quiz: no clip-art, no stock photography, no low-rent quiz-site chrome.
Don't drift into the corporate, sterile official-FIFA-app register.
Don't borrow sportsbook sleaze: no neon-on-black odds-board energy, no aggressive 'bet now'.
Don't ship generic SaaS or AI-startup tropes: no gradient blobs, no identical icon-card grids, no big-number hero-metric template.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text), ever. One solid color.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use glassmorphism as a default surface. The only sanctioned glass is the passport's ticket-stub data band.
Don't use gray drop shadows. Depth is grain, vignette, tonal layering, and colored bloom only.
Don't use #000, #fff, or Inter. Warm cinema-black neutrals and the Stadium type system instead.
Don't use em dashes (or --) in any UI copy.
